questions.question: State and briefly explain the four necessary conditions for a deadlock.
keyPoints: Mutual Exclusion: At least one resource must be non-sharable.
Hold and Wait: A process holds a resource while waiting for another.
No Preemption: Resources cannot be forcibly taken away.
Circular Wait: A circular chain of processes exists, each waiting for a resource held by the next.

questions.question: Describe the Resource-Allocation Graph (RAG) method for deadlock characterization. Explain with an example how a cycle in the graph indicates a deadlock only when involving single-instance resources.
keyPoints: Definition of RAG: A directed graph with processes and resources as nodes.
Explanation of request edges and assignment edges.
Rule: Deadlock exists if graph has a cycle and all resources in cycle are single-instance.
Example of a cycle with single-instance resources leading to deadlock.
Counter-example of a cycle with multi-instance resources where deadlock may not exist.
Conclusion on the sufficiency of a cycle for deadlock detection.
